title: Egyptian Gulf Bank (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for Egyptian Gulf Bank in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is Egyptian Gulf Bank?
faq.answer: Egyptian Gulf Bank (EGBank) is a universal bank in Egypt providing retail, corporate, Islamic, and investment banking services. Founded as a joint venture between Egyptian and Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) banking industries, the bank serves individual consumers, businesses, and institutional clients across Egypt. EGBank is publicly listed on the Egyptian Exchange and operates as one of the country's regional banks with a focus on developmental projects and financial inclusion.

faq.question: When was Egyptian Gulf Bank founded?
faq.answer: Egyptian Gulf Bank was founded on October 14, 1981, under investment law 43/1974 and its amendments. The bank was established by Bukhamseen Holding with the specific mission to support developmental projects in Egypt and other Arab countries. The founding represented a strategic partnership between Egyptian and GCC banking sectors, with Bukhamseen Holding serving as the main shareholder. The bank was publicly listed on the Egyptian Exchange in November 1983, making it one of Egypt's established financial institutions.

faq.question: Where is Egyptian Gulf Bank headquartered?
faq.answer: Egyptian Gulf Bank is headquartered at 8, 10 Ahmed Nessim Street, El Orman Plaza Building, in Giza, Egypt. The bank also maintains operational facilities at 45 North 90th Street, Fifth Settlement, New Cairo.

faq.question: Where does Egyptian Gulf Bank operate?
faq.answer: Egyptian Gulf Bank operates across Egypt through a network of 61 branches strategically located in major urban centers and regional areas. The bank maintains a presence in Cairo, Giza, Alexandria, New Cairo, and Sharm El Sheikh, among other locations. As of 2024, the bank employs approximately 2,352 people serving customers throughout the country.

faq.question: What services does Egyptian Gulf Bank offer?
faq.answer: Egyptian Gulf Bank provides comprehensive banking services across multiple segments: Retail Banking: Personal loans, savings accounts, checking accounts, credit cards, overdraft facilities, certificates of deposit, time deposits, and money transfers. Corporate Banking: Working capital financing, trade finance, project finance, cash management services, letters of credit, guarantees, and business loans. Islamic Banking: Shari'a-compliant deposit accounts, financing products, and investment products approved by the bank's Shari'a supervisory board. Investment Banking: Services through its subsidiary, the Egyptian Gulf Holding Company for Financial Investments, which invests in real estate, tourism, and energy sectors. Digital Banking: Online banking platform, mobile banking application (available on iOS and Android), bill payment through Fawry service integration, IBAN generation, and rewards program.

faq.question: What makes Egyptian Gulf Bank unique?
faq.answer: Egyptian Gulf Bank distinguishes itself through several key initiatives: MINT Youth Banking: The bank operates MINT, a specialized sub-brand targeting Egypt's youth segment with tailored banking products and services designed for younger customers. MINT Incubator: EGBank runs Egypt's leading youth-focused startup accelerator through its MINT Incubator program, supporting entrepreneurship and innovation among young Egyptians. The incubator has facilitated partnerships with Cairo Angels, BznsBuilder, and Geidea Egypt. Fastest Growing Recognition: The bank was named "Fastest Growing Bank in Egypt" in 2016 by The Arab Banks Awards & Commendations of Excellence. Developmental Focus: EGBank maintains its founding mission of supporting developmental projects in infrastructure development, renewable energy, and real estate sectors. Financial Inclusion Leadership: The bank actively participates in Egypt's financial inclusion initiatives, with leadership serving on the Financial Inclusion Committee of the Federation of Egyptian Banks.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is Egyptian Gulf Bank?
faq.answer: Egyptian Gulf Bank is a universal bank (sometimes referred to as a full-service bank in Egypt) that operates across multiple banking segments. As a universal bank, EGBank provides the complete spectrum of financial services including retail banking, corporate banking, Islamic banking, and investment banking activities. The bank is classified as a regional bank within Egypt's banking sector structure and operates as a joint-stock company publicly traded on the Egyptian Exchange under the symbol EGBE. This classification reflects its comprehensive service offerings and multi-segment operational model, which is common among Egypt's larger commercial banking institutions.
